betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Petroil§f

§aEngineered fuel blend from reclaimed oil and lubricant — optimized for diesel generators and radial engines!§f

Petroil is a custom fuel blend produced by mixing §bReclaimed Industrial Oil§f and §bEngine Lubricant§f in the §bIndustrial Mixer§f. With §e130 kTU§f and §e195 kHE§f per bucket, it serves as a medium-grade fuel for the §bDiesel Generator§f and §bRadial Performance Engine§f.

§6Production§f:
§b• Industrial Mixer§f: §e800mB§f Reclaimed Industrial Oil + §e200mB§f Engine Lubricant → §e1,000mB§f Petroil.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Solid Fuel§f: §e13,000mB§f → §e1×§f (Industrial Solidification Machine) or §e5,500mB§f → §e1×§f (Pyrolysis Oven).
§b• Leaded Petroil§f: §e10,000mB§f Petroil + Tetraethyllead → §e12,000mB§f Leaded Petroil.
§b• Fuel§f: For §bDiesel Generator§f and §bRadial Performance Engine§f.
§b• Chainsaw§f: Canisters can be used as fuel.
§b• Tossable Gas Can§f: Craftable from any petroil container.

§eTip§f: Petroil has a relatively low energy density per bucket compared to Diesel or Gasoline, but it can be produced from recycled materials. It is best used as a stepping stone to higher-grade fuels or when you have excess Reclaimed Industrial Oil and Lubricant!
